name: py-redis

metadata.name: py-redis
metadata.summary: A convenience wrapper for the official Python redis package

documentation:
# Py-Redis

A convenience wrapper for the [official Python Redis package](https://pypi.org/project/redis/). 
Supports the Python context manager protocol and takes care of (de-)serializing data to JSON, 
as well as helper methods to work on multiple keys.

## Object Creation

```python
from pyredis import RedisConnection

# pass everything you would pass to redis.Redis()
redis_args = {
    'host': 'localhost',
    # 'password': 'my$ecureRed1s',
    # 'port': 1234,
}

with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    my_redis.set('key', 'value')

```

## Redis Get and Set

```python

# redis set
with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    my_redis.set('a_sting', 'my_sting value')
    my_redis.set('a_list', [1, 4, 3, 2])
    my_redis.set('a_dict', {'key_1': 'val_1', 'key_2': 'val_2'})

# redis get
with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    data = my_redis.get('a_dict')
    # data is already converted to a dict
    print(type(data))

```

## Handle Lists and Dictionaries

```python
from pprint import pprint

# get multiple keys / data
with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    # get all keys that start with a_
    pattern = 'a_'
    keys = my_redis.get_key_pattern(pattern)
    print(f"list of all keys that start with {pattern}: {keys}")
    data = my_redis.get_data_for_keys(keys)
    print(f"data of all keys that start with {pattern}: {data}")

    # or retrieve the data as a key: data dictionary for a specific pattern
    print('data as key: data dictionary for a pattern:')
    data = my_redis.get_keys('a_')
    pprint(data)

# set all entries of a dictionary to redis
data = {'a': 12, 'b': 'myvalue'}
with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    # yo can continue working with the keys
    keys = my_redis.set_dict(data)
    print(my_redis.get('a'))
    print(my_redis.get(keys[1]))
```

## Fallback

```python
# you can always work directly on the redis.Redis() object, 
# as you would with the official package,
# by using the RedisConnection.R attribute
with RedisConnection(**redis_args) as my_redis:
    print('access redis client through object...')
    print(my_redis.R.get('a_dict'))
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.__init__
documentation:
Convenience wrapper class for redis. Passes all arguments directly to redis.

```
param decode_responses

defaults to True for convenience

param args

will be passed to redis.Redis()

param kwargs

will be passed to redis.Redis()
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get_single
documentation:
invokes redis get() method but tries to de-serialize the data to python object.

```
param key

name of the key

return

dict or bytes, depending on whether the de-serialization was successful
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get_multiple
documentation:
invokes get_single(key) for each key in keys.

```
param keys

list of keys

return

list of dicts
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get
documentation:
wraps redis get() method, and takes care of de-serialization. kan handle one or multiple keys

```
param key

key or list of keys
```

\:return dict or list of dicts

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.set
documentation:
invokes redis set() method but serializes python object to json first.

```
param key

name of the key

param data

a json-serializable python object

return str

key, name of the key
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get_keys
documentation:
looks for keys that match `pattern`, retrieve the data,
and return a dictionary of the form key: data.

```
param pattern

key pattern that will be retrieved from redis

return dict

dictionary of the form key: data
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get_key_pattern
documentation:
looks for all keys in redis that fulfil `pattern`

```
param pattern

key pattern that will be retrieved from redis

return list

list of all keys that match the pattern
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.get_data_for_keys
documentation:
returns a list of values for all keys in `keys`

```
param keys

list of keys

return list

list of retrieved data objects
```

methods.name: pyredis.wrapper.RedisConnection.set_dict
documentation:
saves each entry of a python dictionary as a seperate entry to redis,
and returns a list of all the keys that were set.

```
param data

dictionary that should be set to redis

return list

```
